meaning: adj.鏍煎紡涓婄殑,浣撹涓婄殑
examples: There are some stylistic elements in the statue that just don't make sense.
Wilde is also a friend of Yeats, but Yeats' poetry has indeed undergone important and notable stylistic changes.
Yeats sees the Second Coming as an image, as a myth, an idea, a metaphor, a certain stylistic arrangement of experience.
The tango drew on stylistic influences from African, Spanish, Italian and traditional Argentine dance forms.
Across the back runs a slightly polished metal band, the lone bit of stylistic indulgence here.
